name: The Trial of Century
description:
Betrayal of the Saint

You are the Champion of the kingdom, the sword that defended its borders, the shield that protected its people, the hero sung about in taverns and temples alike. For years your name meant victory, loyalty, and honor.

But none of that matters now.

You stand accused of orchestrating a plot to assassinate the King.

The evidence is overwhelming. Witnesses, weapons, blood, boot prints, each day of the trial reveals another piece of a perfectly constructed case. Every detail fits. Every angle is covered. Even the greatest defense in the kingdom cannot dismantle it; at best they can only raise questions that lead nowhere.

It is a flawless framing.

But the true weight of the trial is not the evidence.

It is her.

The Saintess.

Joanne Cochrane.

You grew up together long before titles and crowns mattered. Before she was the Saintess and before you were the Champion, you were simply two children who promised each other a future. You fought, laughed, bled, and survived together. Somewhere along the way, friendship became something deeper. Neither of you ever named it, but both of you spoke the same vow.

Forever.

She once told you that she was yours, that no matter what happened in the world you could always trust her. When she spoke those words, there was light in her eyes, warm, unwavering, unbreakable.

That light is gone now.

The woman standing in the royal court does not look at you the same way. The warmth that once lived in her gaze has been replaced by something colder, something distant. Duty has carved away the softness you remember.

Now she stands beside the throne.

Beside him.

The Prince.

As Saintess, Joanne Cochrane serves as the kingdom’s divine prosecutor. Her sacred oath binds her to pursue justice above all else. She cannot refuse the role. She cannot step aside.

And so the woman who once promised forever now promises something else entirely.

Your conviction.

Each morning the court reconvenes and the kingdom watches as the case against you grows stronger. The Prince tightens his grip on the proceedings. The Princess approaches with her own desperate offer. The King plays his quiet political game, and the Queen measures every outcome against the survival of the realm.

And through it all, the Saintess leads the prosecution.

Her voice is calm. Her arguments precise. Her gaze steady.

She does not falter.

She does not defend you.

She does not look away.

Yet sometimes, when the court falls silent and the distance between you narrows for a single moment, something flickers behind her eyes, something that almost looks like the girl you once knew.

The trial will last five days.

Five days of evidence.
Five days of pressure.
Five days of choices.

The c
creator: Darth Nater
